translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Beginning of the glad tidings of Jesus Christ, Son ofGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as it is written in [Isaiah] the prophet, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, who shall prepare thy way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare the way of [the] Lord, make his paths straight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There came John baptising in the wilderness, and preaching [the] baptism of repentance for remission of sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there went out to him all the district of Judaea, and all they of Jerusalem, and were baptised by him in the river Jordan, confessing their sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And John was clothed in camel's hair, and a leathern girdle about his loins, and ate locusts and wild honey.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he preached, saying, There comes he that is mightier than I after me, the thong of whose sandals I am not fit to stoop down and unloose.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I indeed have baptised you with water, but he shall baptise you with [the] Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass in those days [that] Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptised by John at the Jordan.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And straightway going up from the water, he saw the heavens parting asunder, and the Spirit, as a dove, descending upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there came a voice out of the heavens: Thou art my beloved Son, in thee I have found my delight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately the Spirit drives him out into the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was in the wilderness forty days tempted by Satan, and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But after John was delivered up, Jesus came into Galilee preaching the glad tidings of the kingdom ofGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and saying, The time is fulfilled and the kingdom ofGod has drawn nigh; repent and believe in the glad tidings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And walking by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon, and Andrew, [Simon's] brother, casting out a net in the sea, for they were fishers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, Come after me, and I will make you become fishers of men;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and straightway leaving their trawl-nets they followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going on thence a little, he saw James the [son] of Zebedee, and John his brother, and these [were] in the ship repairing the trawl-nets;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and straightway he called them; and leaving their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, they went away after him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they go into Capernaum. And straightway on the sabbath he entered into the synagogue and taught.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were astonished at his doctrine, for he taught them as having authority, and not as the scribes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was in their synagogue a man [possessed] by an unclean spirit, and he cried out

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Eh! what have we to do with thee, Jesus, Nazarene? Art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the holy one ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace and come out of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the unclean spirit, having torn him, and uttered a cry with a loud voice, came out of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all were amazed, so that they questioned together among themselves, saying, What is this? what new doctrine is this? for with authority he commands even the unclean spirits, and they obey him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his fame went out straightway into the whole region of Galilee around.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And straightway going out of the synagogue, they came with James and John into the house of Simon and Andrew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the mother-in-law of Simon lay in a fever. And straightway they speak to him about her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went up to [her] and raised her up, having taken her by the hand, and straightway the fever left her, and she served them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But evening being come, when the sun had gone down, they brought to him all that were suffering, and those possessed by demons;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the whole city was gathered together at the door.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he healed many suffering from various diseases; and he cast out many demons, and did not suffer the demons to speak because they knew him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And rising in the morning long before day, he went out and went away into a desert place, and there prayed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Simon and those with him went after him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having found him, they say to him, All seek thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Let us go elsewhere into the neighbouring country towns, that I may preach there also, for for this purpose am I come forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was preaching in their synagogues in the whole of Galilee, and casting out demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there comes to him a leper, beseeching him, and falling on his knees to him, and saying to him, If thou wilt thou canst cleanse me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, moved with compassion, having stretched out his hand, touched him, and says to him, I will, be thou cleansed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he spoke straightway the leprosy left him, and he was cleansed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having sharply charged him, he straightway sent him away,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and says to him, See thou say nothing to any one, but go, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing what Moses ordained, for a testimony to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, having gone forth, began to proclaim [it] much, and to spread the matter abroad, so that he could no longer enter openly into the city, but was without in desert places, and they came to him from every side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he entered again into Capernaum after [several] days, and it was reported that he was at [the] house;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and straightway many were gathered together, so that there was no longer any room, not even at the door; and he spoke the word to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there come to him [men] bringing a paralytic, borne by four;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, not being able to get near to him on account of the crowd, they uncovered the roof where he was, and having dug [it] up they let down the couch on which the paralytic lay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, seeing their faith, says to the paralytic, Child, thy sins are forgiven [thee].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But certain of the scribes were there sitting, and reasoning in their hearts,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Why does this [man] thus speak? he blasphemes. Who is able to forgive sins exceptGod alone?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And straightway Jesus, knowing in his spirit that they are reasoning thus within themselves, said to them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Which is easier, to say to the paralytic, [Thy] sins are forgiven [thee]; or to say, Arise, and take up thy couch and walk?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But that ye may know that the Son of man has power on earth to forgive sins, he says to the paralytic,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To thee I say, Arise, take up thy couch and go to thine house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rose up straightway, and, having taken up his couch, went out before [them] all, so that all were amazed, and glorifiedGod, saying, We never saw it thus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went out again by the sea, and all the crowd came to him, and he taught them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And passing by, he saw Levi the [son] of Alphaeus sitting at the tax-office, and says to him, Follow me. And he rose up and followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he lay at table in his house, that many tax-gatherers and sinners lay at table with Jesus and his disciples; for they were many, and they followed him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes and the Pharisees, seeing him eating with sinners and tax-gatherers, said to his disciples, Why [is it] that he eats and drinks with tax-gatherers and sinners?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus having heard [it] says to them, They that are strong have not need of a physician, but those who are ill. I have not come to call righteous [men], but sinners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples of John and the Pharisees were fasting; and they come and say to him, Why do the disciples of John and [the disciples] of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, Can the sons of the bride-chamber fast while the bridegroom is with them? As long as they have the bridegroom with them they cannot fast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But days will come when the bridegroom shall have been taken away from them, and then shall they fast in that day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: No one sews a patch of new cloth on an old garment: otherwise its new filling-up takes from the old [stuff], and there is a worse rent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And no one puts new wine into old skins; otherwise the wine bursts the skins, and the wine is poured out, and the skins will be destroyed; but new wine is to be put into new skins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass that he went on the sabbath through the cornfields; and his disciples began to walk on, plucking the ears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees said to him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath what is not lawful?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Have ye never read what David did when he had need and hungered, he and those with him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: how he entered into the house ofGod, in [the section of] Abiathar [the] high priest, and ate the shew-bread, which it is not lawful unless for the priests to eat, and gave even to those that were with him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, The sabbath was made on account of man, not man on account of the sabbath;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so that the Son of man is lord of the sabbath also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was there a man having his hand dried up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they watched him if he would heal him on the sabbath, that they might accuse him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to the man who had his hand dried up, Rise up [and come] into the midst.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Is it lawful on the sabbath to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill? But they were silent.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And looking round upon them with anger, distressed at the hardening of their heart, he says to the man, Stretch out thy hand. And he stretched [it] out, and his hand was restored.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees going out straightway with the Herodians took counsel against him, how they might destroy him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the sea; and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judaea,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea and beyond the Jordan; and they of around Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, having heard what things he did, came to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke to his disciples, in order that a little ship should wait upon him on account of the crowd, that they might not press upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he healed many, so that they beset him that they might touch him, as many as had plagues.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the unclean spirits, when they beheld him, fell down before him, and cried saying, Thou art the Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rebuked them much, that they might not make him manifest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he goes up into the mountain, and calls whom he himself would, and they went to him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he appointed twelve that they might be with him, and that he might send them to preach,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to have power [to heal diseases, and] to cast out demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he gave to Simon the surname of Peter;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and James the [son] of Zebedee, and John the brother of James, and he gave them the surname of Boanerges, that is, Sons of thunder;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the [son] of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Judas Iscariote, who also delivered him up. And they come to [the] house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again a crowd comes together, so that they cannot even eat bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his relatives having heard [of it] went out to lay hold on him, for they said, He is out of his mind.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribes who had come down from Jerusalem said, He has Beelzebub, and, By the prince of the demons he casts out demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called them to [him], he said to them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a kingdom has become divided against itself, that kingdom cannot subsist.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a house has become divided against itself, that house cannot subsist.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if Satan rise up against himself, and is divided, he cannot subsist, but has an end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But no one can, having entered into his house, plunder the goods of the strong [man] unless he first bind the strong [man], and then he will plunder his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say unto you, that all sins shall be forgiven to the sons of men, and all the injurious speeches [with] which they may speak injuriously;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but whosoever shall speak injuriously against the Holy Spirit, to eternity has no forgiveness; but lies under the guilt of an everlasting sin;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — because they said, He has an unclean spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his brethren and his mother come, and standing without sent to him calling him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a crowd sat around him. And they said to him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren seek thee without.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother or my brethren?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And looking around in a circuit at those that were sitting around him, he says, Behold my mother and my brethren:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for whosoever shall do the will ofGod, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again he began to teach by the sea. And a great crowd was gathered together to him, so that going on board ship he sat in the sea, and all the crowd were close to the sea on the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he taught them many things in parables. And he said to them in his doctrine,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Hearken: Behold, the sower went forth to sow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it came to pass as he sowed, one fell by the wayside, and the birds came and devoured it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And another fell on the rocky ground, where it had not much earth, and immediately it sprung up out [of the ground] because it had no depth of earth;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when the sun arose it was burnt up, and because of its not having any root, it withered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And another fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And another fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing; and bore, one thirty, and one sixty, and one a hundred.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, He that has ears to hear, let him hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was alone, those about him with the twelve asked him [as to] the parables.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, To you is given [to know] the mystery of the kingdom ofGod; but to them who are without, all things are done in parables,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that beholding they may behold and not see, and hearing they may hear and not understand, lest it may be, they should be converted and they should be forgiven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Do ye not know this parable? and how will ye be acquainted with all the parables?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sower sows the word:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and these are they by the wayside where the word is sown, and when they hear, immediately Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are they in like manner who are sown upon the rocky places, who when they hear the word, immediately receive it with joy,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they have no root in themselves, but are for a time: then, tribulation arising, or persecution on account of the word, immediately they are offended.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And others are they who are sown among the thorns: these are they who have heard the word,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the cares of life, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things, entering in, choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are they who have been sown on the good ground, such as hear the word and receive it, and bear fruit; one thirty, and one sixty, and one a hundred [fold].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Does the lamp come that it should be put under the bushel or under the couch? [Is it] not that it should be set upon the lamp-stand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there is nothing hidden which shall not be made manifest; nor does any secret thing take place, but that it should come to light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one have ears to hear, let him hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Take heed what ye hear; with what measure ye mete, it shall be meted to you; and there shall be [more] added to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whosoever has, to him shall be given; and he who has not, even what he has shall be taken from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Thus is the kingdom ofGod, as if a man should cast the seed upon the earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and should sleep and rise up night and day, and the seed should sprout and grow, he does not know how.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The earth bears fruit of itself, first [the] blade, then an ear, then full corn in the ear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when the fruit is produced, immediately he sends the sickle, for the harvest is come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, How should we liken the kingdom ofGod, or with what comparison should we compare it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As to a grain of mustard [seed], which, when it is sown upon the earth, is less than all seeds which are upon the earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and when it has been sown, mounts up and becomes greater than all herbs, and produces great branches, so that the birds of heaven can roost under its shadow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but without a parable spoke he not to them; and in private he explained all things to his disciples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on that day, when evening was come, he says to them, Let us go over to the other side:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having sent away the crowd, they take him with [them], as he was, in the ship. But other ships also were with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there comes a violent gust of wind, and the waves beat into the ship, so that it already filled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he was in the stern sleeping on the cushion. And they awake him up and say to him, Teacher, dost thou not care that we are perishing?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And awaking up he rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, Silence; be mute. And the wind fell, and there was a great calm.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Why are ye [thus] fearful? how [is it] ye have not faith?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they feared [with] great fear, and said one to another, Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they came to the other side of the sea, to the country of the Gadarenes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately on his going out of the ship there met him out of the tombs a man possessed by an unclean spirit,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who had his dwelling in the tombs; and no one was able to bind him, not even with chains;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been torn asunder by him, and the fetters were shattered; and no one was able to subdue him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And continually night and day, in the tombs and in the mountains, he was crying and cutting himself with stones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But seeing Jesus from afar off, he ran and did him homage,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and crying with a loud voice he says, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, Son of the Most HighGod? I adjure thee byGod, torment me not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he said to him, Come forth, unclean spirit, out of the man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he asked him, What is thy name? And he says to him, Legion is my name, because we are many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he besought him much that he would not send them away out of the country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there was there just at the mountain a great herd of swine feeding;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they besought him, saying, Send us into the swine that we may enter into them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus [immediately] allowed them. And the unclean spirits going out entered into the swine, and the herd rushed down the steep slope, into the sea (about two thousand), and were choked in the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that were feeding them fled and reported it in the city and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that had taken place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they come to Jesus, and they see the possessed of demons sitting [and] clothed and sensible, [him] that had had the legion: and they were afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that had seen [it] related to them how it had happened to the [man] possessed by demons, and concerning the swine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they began to beg him to depart from their coasts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he went on board ship, the man that had been possessed by demons besought him that he might be with him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he suffered him not, but says to him, Go to thine home to thine own people, and tell them how great things the Lord has done for thee, and has had mercy on thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went away and began to proclaim in the Decapolis how great things Jesus had done for him; and all wondered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus having passed over in the ship again to the other side, a great crowd gathered to him; and he was by the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [behold] there comes one of the rulers of the synagogue, by name Jairus, and seeing him, falls down at his feet;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he besought him much, saying, My little daughter is at extremity; [I pray] that thou shouldest come and lay thy hands upon her so that she may be healed, and may live.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went with him, and a large crowd followed him and pressed on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain woman who had had a flux of blood twelve years,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and had suffered much under many physicians, and had spent everything she had and had found no advantage from it, but had rather got worse,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: having heard concerning Jesus, came in the crowd behind and touched his clothes;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for she said, If I shall touch but his clothes I shall be healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately her fountain of blood was dried up, and she knew in her body that she was cured from the scourge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately Jesus, knowing in himself the power that had gone out of him, turning round in the crowd said, Who has touched my clothes?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples said to him, Thou seest the crowd pressing on thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he looked round about to see her who had done this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the woman, frightened and trembling, knowing what had taken place in her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, Daughter, thy faith has healed thee; go in peace, and be well of thy scourge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: While he was yet speaking, they come from the ruler of the synagogue's [house], saying, Thy daughter has died, why troublest thou the teacher any further?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus [immediately], having heard the word spoken, says to the ruler of the synagogue, Fear not; only believe.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he suffered no one to accompany him save Peter and James, and John the brother of James.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he comes to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and sees the tumult, and people weeping and wailing greatly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And entering in he says to them, Why do ye make a tumult and weep? the child has not died, but sleeps.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they derided him. But he, having put [them] all out, takes with [him] the father of the child, and the mother, and those that were with him, and enters in where the child was lying.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having laid hold of the hand of the child, he says to her, Talitha koumi, which is, interpreted, Damsel, I say to thee, Arise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately the damsel arose and walked, for she was twelve years old. And they were astonished with great astonishment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he charged them much that no one should know this; and he desired that [something] should be given her to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went out thence and came to his own country, and his disciples follow him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when sabbath was come he began to teach in the synagogue, and many hearing were amazed, saying, Whence [has] this [man] these things? and what [is] the wisdom that is given to him, and such works of power are done by his hands?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary, and brother of James, and Joses, and Judas, and Simon? and are not his sisters here with us? And they were offended in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to them, A prophet is not despised save in his own country, and among [his] kinsmen, and in his own house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he could not do any work of power there, save that laying his hands on a few infirm persons he healed [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he wondered because of their unbelief. And he went round the villages in a circuit, teaching.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he calls the twelve to [him]; and he began to send them out two [and] two, and gave to them power over the unclean spirits;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he commanded them that they should take nothing for the way, save a staff only; no scrip, no bread, no money in their belt;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but be shod with sandals, and put not on two body-coats.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Wheresoever ye shall enter into a house, there remain till ye shall go thence.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whatsoever place shall not receive you nor hear you, departing thence, shake off the dust which is under your feet for a testimony to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went forth and preached that they should repent;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they cast out many demons, and anointed with oil many infirm, and healed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Herod the king heard [of him] (for his name had become public), and said, John the baptist is risen from among [the] dead, and on this account works of power are wrought by him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And others said, It is Elias; and others said, It is a prophet, as one of the prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Herod when he heard [it] said, John whom I beheaded, he it is; he is risen [from among the dead].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the same Herod had sent and seized John, and had bound him in prison on account of Herodias, the wife of Philip his brother, because he had married her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For John said to Herod, It is not lawful for thee to have the wife of thy brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Herodias kept it [in her mind] against him, and wished to kill him, and could not:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for Herod feared John knowing that he was a just and holy man, and kept him safe; and having heard him, did many things, and heard him gladly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a holiday being come, when Herod, on his birthday, made a supper to his grandees, and to the chiliarchs, and the chief [men] of Galilee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the daughter of the same Herodias having come in, and danced, pleased Herod and those that were with [him] at table; and the king said to the damsel, Ask of me whatsoever thou wilt and I will give it thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he swore to her, Whatsoever thou shalt ask me I will give thee, to half of my kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And she went out, and said to her mother, What should I ask? And she said, The head of John the baptist.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately going in with haste to the king, she asked saying, I desire that thou give me directly upon a dish the head of John the baptist.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the king, [while] made very sorry, on account of the oaths and those lying at table with [him] would not break his word with her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately the king, having sent one of the guard, ordered his head to be brought. And he went out and beheaded him in the prison,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and brought his head upon a dish, and gave it to the damsel, and the damsel gave it to her mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples having heard [it], came and took up his body, and laid it in a tomb.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the apostles are gathered together to Jesus. And they related to him all things, [both] what they had done and what they had taught.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place and rest a little. For those coming and those going were many, and they had not leisure even to eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went away apart into a desert place by ship.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And many saw them going, and recognised them, and ran together there on foot, out of all the cities, and got [there] before them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on leaving [the ship] [Jesus] saw a great crowd, and he was moved with compassion for them, because they were as sheep not having a shepherd. And he began to teach them many things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was already late in the day, his disciples coming to him say, The place is desert, and it is already late in the day;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: send them away that they may go into the country and villages around, and buy themselves bread, for they have not anything they can eat.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to them, Give ye them to eat. And they say to him, Shall we go and buy two hundred denarii worth of bread and give them to eat?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, How many loaves have ye? Go [and] see. And when they knew they say, Five, and two fishes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he ordered them to make them all sit down by companies on the green grass.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sat down in ranks by hundreds and by fifties.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken the five loaves and the two fishes, looking up to heaven, he blessed, and broke the loaves, and gave [them] to his disciples that they might set [them] before them. And the two fishes he divided among all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they all ate and were satisfied.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took up of fragments the fillings of twelve hand-baskets, and of the fishes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those that ate of the loaves were five thousand men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately he compelled his disciples to go on board ship, and to go on before to the other side to Bethsaida, while he sends the crowd away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, having dismissed them, he departed into the mountain to pray.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when evening was come, the ship was in the midst of the sea, and he alone upon the land.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seeing them labouring in rowing, for the wind was contrary to them, about the fourth watch of the night he comes to them walking on the sea, and would have passed them by.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they, seeing him walking on the sea, thought that it was an apparition, and cried out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For all saw him and were troubled. And immediately he spoke with them, and says to them, Be of good courage: it is I; be not afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he went up to them into the ship, and the wind fell. And they were exceedingly beyond measure astonished in themselves and wondered;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for they understood not through the loaves: for their heart was hardened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having passed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret and made the shore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on their coming out of the ship, immediately recognising him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they ran through that whole country around, and began to carry about those that were ill on couches, where they heard that he was.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And wherever he entered into villages, or cities, or the country, they laid the sick in the market-places, and besought him that they might touch if it were only the hem of his garment; and as many as touched him were healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees and some of the scribes, coming from Jerusalem, are gathered together to him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and seeing some of his disciples eat bread with defiled, that is, unwashed, hands,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for the Pharisees and all the Jews, unless they wash their hands diligently, do not eat, holding what has been delivered by the ancients;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and [on coming] from the market-place, unless they are washed, they do not eat; and there are many other things which they have received to hold, the washing of cups and vessels, and brazen utensils, and couches),

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then the Pharisees and the scribes ask him, Why do thy disciples not walk according to what has been delivered by the ancients, but eat the bread with defiled hands?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said to them, Well did Esaias prophesy concerning you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honour me with their lips, but their heart is far away from me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in vain do they worship me, teaching [as their] teachings commandments of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [For], leaving the commandment ofGod, ye hold what is delivered by men [to keep] — washings of vessels and cups, and many other such like things ye do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Well do ye set aside the commandment ofGod, that ye may observe what is delivered by yourselves [to keep].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, he who speaks ill of father or mother, let him surely die.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye say, If a man say to his father or his mother, [It is] corban (that is, gift), whatsoever thou mightest have profit from me by...

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye no longer suffer him to do anything for his father or his mother;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: making void the word ofGod by your traditional teaching which ye have delivered; and many such like things ye do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called again the crowd, he said to them, Hear me, all [of you], and understand:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There is nothing from outside a man entering into him which can defile him; but the things which go out from him, those it is which defile the man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If any one have ears to hear, let him hear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he went indoors from the crowd, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Are ye also thus unintelligent? Do ye not perceive that all that is outside entering into the man cannot defile him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: because it does not enter into his heart but into his belly, and goes out into the draught, purging all meats?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, That which goes forth out of the man, that defiles the man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For from within, out of the heart of men, go forth evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thefts, covetousness, wickednesses, deceit, licentiousness, a wicked eye, injurious language, haughtiness, folly;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: all these wicked things go forth from within and defile the man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he rose up and went away thence into the borders of Tyre and Sidon; and having entered into a house he would not have any one know [it], and he could not be hid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But immediately a woman, whose little daughter had an unclean spirit, having heard of him, came and fell at his feet

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (and the woman was a Greek, Syrophenician by race), and asked him that he would cast the demon out of her daughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But [Jesus] said to her, Suffer the children to be first filled; for it is not right to take the children's bread and cast it to the dogs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But she answered and says to him, Yea, Lord; for even the dogs under the table eat of the children's crumbs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to her, Because of this word, go thy way, the demon is gone out of thy daughter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having gone away to her house she found the demon gone out, and her daughter lying on the bed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again having left the borders of Tyre and Sidon, he came to the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they bring to him a deaf [man] who could not speak right, and they beseech him that he might lay his hand on him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken him away from the crowd apart, he put his fingers to his ears; and having spit, he touched his tongue;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and looking up to heaven he groaned, and says to him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately his ears were opened, and the band of his tongue was loosed and he spoke right.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he charged them that they should speak to no one [of it]. But so much the more he charged them, so much the more abundantly they proclaimed it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they were astonished above measure, saying, He does all things well; he makes both the deaf to hear, and the speechless to speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In those days, there being again a great crowd, and they having nothing that they could eat, having called his disciples to [him], he says to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have compassion on the crowd, because they have stayed with me already three days and they have not anything they can eat,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if I should dismiss them to their home fasting, they will faint on the way; for some of them are come from far.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples answered him, Whence shall one be able to satisfy these with bread here in a desert place?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he commanded the crowd to sit down on the ground. And having taken the seven loaves, he gave thanks, and broke [them] and gave [them] to his disciples, that they might set [them] before [them]; and they set [them] before the crowd.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they had a few small fishes, and having blessed them, he desired these also to be set before [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they ate and were satisfied. And they took up of fragments that remained seven baskets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they [that had eaten] were about four thousand; and he sent them away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately going on board ship with his disciples, he came into the parts of Dalmanutha.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Pharisees went out and began to dispute against him, seeking from him a sign from heaven, tempting him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And groaning in his spirit, he says, Why does this generation seek a sign? Verily I say unto you, A sign shall in no wise be given to this generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he left them, and going again on board ship, went away to the other side.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they forgot to take bread, and save one loaf, they had not [any] with them in the ship.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and [of] the leaven of Herod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they reasoned with one another, [saying], It is because we have no bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus knowing [it], says to them, Why reason ye because ye have no bread? Do ye not yet perceive nor understand? Have ye your heart [yet] hardened?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: When I broke the five loaves for the five thousand, how many hand-baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say to him, Twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the seven for the four thousand, the filling of how many baskets of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, How do ye not yet understand?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he comes to Bethsaida; and they bring him a blind man, and beseech him that he might touch him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And taking hold of the hand of the blind man he led him forth out of the village, and having spit upon his eyes, he laid his hands upon him, and asked him if he beheld anything.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having looked up, he said, I behold men, for I see [them], as trees, walking.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then he laid his hands again upon his eyes, and he saw distinctly, and was restored and saw all things clearly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent him to his house, saying, Neither enter into the village, nor tell [it] to any one in the village.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus went forth and his disciples, into the villages of Caesarea-Philippi. And by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Who do men say that I am?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they answered him, saying, John the baptist; and others, Elias; but others, One of the prophets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he asked them, But ye, who do ye say that I am? And Peter answering says to him, Thou art the Christ.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he charged them straitly, in order that they should tell no man about him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he began to teach them that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders and of the chief priests and of the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise [again].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he spoke the thing openly. And Peter, taking him to [him], began to rebuke him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, turning round and seeing his disciples, rebuked Peter, saying, Get away behind me, Satan, for thy mind is not on the things that are ofGod, but on the things that are of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called the crowd with his disciples, he said to them, Whoever desires to come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross and follow me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whosoever shall desire to save his life shall lose it, but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel's shall save it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For what shall it profit a man if he gain the whole world and suffer the loss of his soul?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for what should a man give in exchange for his soul?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whosoever shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation, of him shall the Son of man also be ashamed when he shall come in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Verily I say unto you, There are some of those standing here that shall not taste death until they shall have seen the kingdom ofGod come in power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after six days Jesus takes with [him] Peter and James and John, and takes them up on a high mountain by themselves apart. And he was transfigured before them:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and his garments became shining, exceeding white [as snow], such as fuller on earth could not whiten [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there appeared to them Elias with Moses, and they were talking with Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter answering says to Jesus, Rabbi, it is good that we should be here; and let us make three tabernacles, for thee one, and for Moses one, and for Elias one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he knew not what he should say, for they were filled with fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there came a cloud overshadowing them, and there came a voice out of the cloud, This is my beloved Son: hear him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And suddenly having looked around, they no longer saw any one, but Jesus alone with themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they descended from the mountain, he charged them that they should relate to no one what they had seen, unless when the Son of man should be risen from among [the] dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they kept that saying, questioning among themselves, what rising from among [the] dead was.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they asked him saying, Why do the scribes say that Elias must first have come?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to them, Elias indeed, having first come, restores all things; and how is it written of the Son of man that he must suffer much, and be set at nought:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but I say unto you that Elias also is come, and they have done to him whatever they would, as it is written of him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he came to the disciples he saw a great crowd around them, and scribes disputing against them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately all the crowd seeing him were amazed, and running to [him], saluted him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he asked them, What do ye question with them about?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one out of the crowd answered him, Teacher, I brought to thee my son, who has a dumb spirit;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and wheresoever it seizes him it tears him, and he foams and gnashes his teeth, and he is withering away. And I spoke to thy disciples, that they might cast him out, and they could not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering them says, O unbelieving generation! how long shall I be with you? how long shall I bear with you? bring him to me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought him to him. And seeing him the spirit immediately tore him; and falling upon the earth he rolled foaming.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he asked his father, How long a time is it that it has been like this with him? And he said, From childhood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and often it has cast him both into fire and into waters that it might destroy him: but if thou couldst [do] anything, be moved with pity on us, and help us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, The 'if thou couldst' is [if thou couldst] believe: all things are possible to him that believes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately the father of the young child crying out said [with tears], I believe, help mine unbelief.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, seeing that [the] crowd was running up together, rebuked the unclean spirit, saying to him, Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I command thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having cried out and torn [him] much, he came out; and he became as if dead, so that the most said, He is dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus, having taken hold of him by the hand, lifted him up, and he arose.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was entered into the house, his disciples asked him privately, Wherefore could not we cast him out?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, This kind can go out by nothing but by prayer and fasting.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going forth from thence they went through Galilee; and he would not that any one knew it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he taught his disciples and said to them, The Son of man is delivered into men's hands, and they shall kill him; and having been killed, after three days he shall rise again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they understood not the saying, and feared to ask him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to Capernaum, and being in the house, he asked them, Of what were ye reasoning by the way?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they remained silent, for by the way they had been reasoning with one another who [was] greatest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And sitting down he called the twelve; and he says to them, If any one would be first, he shall be last of all, and minister of all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And taking a little child he set it in their midst, and having taken it in his arms he said to them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whosoever shall receive one of such little children in my name, receives me; and whosoever shall receive me, does not receive me, but him who sent me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And John answered him saying, Teacher, we saw some one casting out demons in thy name, who does not follow us, and we forbad him, because he does not follow us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said, Forbid him not; for there is no one who shall do a miracle in my name, and be able soon [after] to speak ill of me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he who is not against us is for us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in [my] name, because ye are Christ's, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whosoever shall be a snare to one of the little ones who believe [in me], it were better for him if a millstone were hung about his neck, and he cast into the sea.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thy hand serve as a snare to thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having thy two hands to go away into hell, into the fire unquenchable;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [where their worm dies not, and the fire is not quenched].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thy foot serve as a snare to thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life lame, than having thy two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire unquenchable;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [where their worm dies not, and the fire is not quenched].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if thine eye serve as a snare to thee, cast it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom ofGod with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the hell of fire,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: where their worm dies not, and the fire is not quenched.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every one shall be salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Salt [is] good, but if the salt is become saltless, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and be at peace with one another.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And rising up thence he comes into the coasts of Judaea, and the other side of the Jordan. And again crowds come together to him, and, as he was accustomed, again he taught them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pharisees coming to [him] asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away [his] wife? (tempting him).

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answering said to them, What did Moses command you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said, Moses allowed to write a bill of divorce, and to put away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, In view of your hard-heartedness he wrote this commandment for you;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but from [the] beginning of [the] creationGod made them male and female.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this cause a man shall leave his father and mother and shall be united to his wife,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the two shall be one flesh: so that they are no longer two but one flesh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What thereforeGod has joined together, let not man separate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again in the house the disciples asked him concerning this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, Whosoever shall put away his wife and shall marry another, commits adultery against her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if a woman put away her husband and shall marry another, she commits adultery.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought little children to him that he might touch them. But the disciples rebuked those that brought [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus seeing [it], was indignant, and said to them, Suffer the little children to come to me; forbid them not; for of such is the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom ofGod as a little child, shall in no wise enter into it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken them in his arms, having laid his hands on them, he blessed them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he went forth into the way, a person ran up to [him], and kneeling to him asked him, Good Teacher, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said to him, Why callest thou me good? no one is good but one, [that is]God.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou knowest the commandments: Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Do not defraud, Honour thy father and mother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he answering said to him, Teacher, all these things have I kept from my youth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus looking upon him loved him, and said to him, One thing lackest thou: go, sell whatever thou hast and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me, [taking up the cross].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, sad at the word, went away grieved, for he had large possessions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus looking around says to his disciples, How difficultly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom ofGod!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the disciples were amazed at his words. And Jesus again answering says to them, Children, how difficult it is that those who trust in riches should enter into the kingdom ofGod!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were exceedingly astonished, saying to one another, And who can be saved?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus looking on them says, With men it is impossible, but not withGod; for all things are possible withGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Peter began to say to him, Behold, we have left all things and have followed thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus answering said, Verily I say to you, There is no one who has left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, [or wife], or children, or lands, for my sake and for the sake of the gospel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that shall not receive a hundredfold now in this time: houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions, and in the coming age life eternal.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But many first shall be last, and the last first.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem, and Jesus was going on before them; and they were amazed, and were afraid as they followed. And taking the twelve again to [him], he began to tell them what was going to happen to him:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold, we go up to Jerusalem, and the Son of man shall be delivered up to the chief priests and to the scribes, and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him up to the nations:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him; and after three days he shall rise again.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there come to him James and John, the sons of Zebedee, saying [to him], Teacher, we would that whatsoever we may ask thee, thou wouldst do it for us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, What would ye that I should do for you?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, Give to us that we may sit, one on thy right hand, and one on thy left hand, in thy glory.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to them, Ye do not know what ye ask. Are ye able to drink the cup which I drink, or be baptised with the baptism that I am baptised with?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to him, We are able. And Jesus said to them, The cup that I drink ye will drink and with the baptism that I am baptised with ye will be baptised,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but to sit on my right hand or on my left is not mine to give, but for those for whom it is prepared.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the ten having heard [of it], began to be indignant about James and John.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus having called them to [him], says to them, Ye know that those who are esteemed to rule over the nations exercise lordship over them; and their great men exercise authority over them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but it is not thus among you; but whosoever would be great among you, shall be your minister;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and whosoever would be first of you shall be bondman of all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For also the Son of man did not come to be ministered to, but to minister, and give his life a ransom for many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they come to Jericho, and as he was going out from Jericho, and his disciples and a large crowd, the son of Timaeus, Bartimaeus, the blind [man], sat by the wayside begging.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having heard that it was Jesus the Nazaraean, he began to cry out and to say, O Son of David, Jesus, have mercy on me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And many rebuked him, that he might be silent; but he cried so much the more, Son of David, have mercy on me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, standing still, desired him to be called. And they call the blind [man], saying to him, Be of good courage, rise up, he calls thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, throwing away his garment, he started up and came to Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering says to him, What wilt thou that I shall do to thee? And the blind [man] said to him, Rabboni, that I may see.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said to him, Go, thy faith has healed thee. And he saw immediately, and followed him in the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they draw near to Jerusalem, to Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount of Olives, he sends two of his disciples,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and says to them, Go into the village which is over against you, and immediately on entering into it ye will find a colt tied, upon which no [child] of man has ever sat: loose it and lead it [here].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if any one say to you, Why do ye this? say, The Lord has need of it; and straightway he sends it hither.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they departed, and found a colt bound to the door without at the crossway, and they loose him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of those who stood there said to them, What are ye doing, loosing the colt?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to them as Jesus had commanded. And they let them [do it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they led the colt to Jesus, and cast their clothes upon it, and he sat on it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and many strewed their clothes on the way, and others cut down branches from the trees [and went on strewing them on the way].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And those going on before and those following cried out, Hosanna! blessed [be] he that comes in [the] Lord's name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed [be] the coming kingdom of our father David. Hosanna in the highest!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he entered into Jerusalem and into the temple; and having looked round on all things, the hour being already late, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the morrow, when they were gone out of Bethany, he hungered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And seeing from afar off a fig-tree which had leaves, he came, if perhaps he might find something on it. And having come up to it he found nothing but leaves, for it was not the time of figs.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And answering he said to it, Let no one eat fruit of thee any more for ever. And his disciples heard [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they come to Jerusalem, and entering into the temple, he began to cast out those who sold and who bought in the temple, and he overthrew the tables of the moneychangers and the seats of the dove-sellers,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and suffered not that any one should carry any package through the temple.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he taught saying to them, Is it not written, My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations? but ye have made it a den of robbers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests and the scribes heard [it], and they sought how they might destroy him; for they feared him, because all the crowd were astonished at his doctrine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was evening he went forth without the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And passing by early in the morning they saw the fig-tree dried up from the roots.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter, remembering [what Jesus had said], says to him, Rabbi, see, the fig-tree which thou cursedst is dried up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering says to them, Have faith inGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to you, that whosoever shall say to this mountain, Be thou taken away and cast into the sea, and shall not doubt in his heart, but believe that what he says takes place, whatever he shall say shall come to pass for him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this reason I say to you, All things whatsoever ye pray for and ask, believe that ye receive it, and it shall come to pass for you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when ye stand praying, forgive if ye have anything against any one, that your Father also who is in the heavens may forgive you your offences.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye do not forgive, neither will your Father who is in the heavens forgive your offences.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they come again to Jerusalem. And as he walked about in the temple, the chief priests and the scribes and the elders come to him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they say to him, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority, that thou shouldest do these things?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, I also will ask you one thing, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The baptism of John, was it of heaven, or of men? answer me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we should say, Of heaven, he will say, Why [then] have ye not believed him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but should we say, Of men — they feared the people; for all held of John that he was truly a prophet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they answering say to Jesus, We do not know. And Jesus [answering] says to them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he began to say to them in parables, A man planted a vineyard, and made a fence round [it] and dug a wine-vat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and left the country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sent a bondman to the husbandmen at the season, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But they took him, and beat [him], and sent [him] away empty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again he sent to them another bondman; and [at] him they [threw stones, and] struck [him] on the head, and sent [him] away with insult.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And [again] he sent another, and him they killed; and many others, beating some and killing some.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having yet therefore one beloved son, he sent also him to them the last, saying, They will have respect for my son.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But those husbandmen said to one another, This is the heir: come, let us kill him and the inheritance will be ours.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they took him and killed him, and cast him forth out of the vineyard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the vineyard to others.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Have ye not even read this scripture, The stone which they that builded rejected, this has become the corner-stone:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: this is of [the] Lord, and it is wonderful in our eyes?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sought to lay hold of him, and they feared the crowd; for they knew that he had spoken the parable of them. And they left him and went away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they send to him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, that they might catch him in speaking.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they come and say to him, Teacher, we know that thou art true, and carest not for any one; for thou regardest not men's person, but teachest the way ofGod with truth: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar or not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Should we give, or should we not give? But he knowing their hypocrisy said unto them, Why tempt ye me? Bring me a denarius that I may see [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they brought [it]. And he says to them, Whose [is] this image and superscription? And they said to him, Caesar's.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, Pay what is Caesar's to Caesar, and what isGod's toGod. And they wondered at him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Sadducees come to him, that say there is no resurrection; and they demanded of him saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if any one's brother die, and leave a wife behind, and leave no children, that his brother shall take his wife, and raise up seed to his brother.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There were seven brethren; and the first took a wife, and dying did not leave seed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the second took her and died, and neither did he leave seed; and the third likewise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
