translations.text: And the seven [took her and] did not leave seed. Last of all the woman also died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the resurrection, when they shall rise again, of which of them shall she be wife, for the seven had her as wife?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, Do not ye therefore err, not knowing the scriptures, nor the power ofGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For when they rise from among [the] dead they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are as angels [who are] in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But concerning the dead that they rise, have ye not read in the book of Moses, in [the section of] the bush, howGod spoke to him, saying, I [am] theGod of Abraham, and theGod of Isaac, and theGod of Jacob?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is not theGod of [the] dead, but of [the] living. Ye therefore greatly err.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one of the scribes who had come up, and had heard them reasoning together, perceiving that he had answered them well, demanded of him, Which is [the] first commandment of all?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answered him, [The] first commandment of all [is], Hear, Israel: the Lord ourGod is one Lord;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thou shalt love the Lord thyGod with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thine understanding, and with all thy strength. This is [the] first commandment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a second like it [is] this: Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. There is not another commandment greater than these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the scribe said to him, Right, teacher; thou hast spoken according to [the] truth. For he is one, and there is none other besides him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to love him with all the heart, and with all the intelligence, and with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love one's neighbour as one's self, is more than all the burnt-offerings and sacrifices.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, seeing that he had answered intelligently, said to him, Thou art not far from the kingdom ofGod. And no one dared question him any more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said [as he was] teaching in the temple, How do the scribes say that the Christ is son of David?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [for] David himself said [speaking] in the Holy Spirit, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit on my right hand until I put thine enemies [as] footstool of thy feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: David himself [therefore] calls him Lord, and whence is he his son? And the mass of the people heard him gladly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them in his doctrine, Beware of the scribes, who like to walk about in long robes, and salutations in the marketplaces,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and first seats in the synagogues, and first places at suppers;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who devour the houses of widows, and as a pretext make long prayers. These shall receive a severer judgment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, having sat down opposite the treasury, saw how the crowd was casting money into the treasury; and many rich cast in much.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a poor widow came and cast in two mites, which is a farthing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having called his disciples to [him] he said to them, Verily I say unto you, This poor widow has cast in more than all who have cast into the treasury:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for all have cast in of that which they had in abundance, but she of her destitution has cast in all that she had, the whole of her living.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he was going out of the temple, one of his disciples says to him, Teacher, see what stones and what buildings!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to him, Seest thou these great buildings? not a stone shall be left upon a stone, which shall not be thrown down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as he sat on the mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Tell us, when shall these things be, and what is the sign when all these things are going to be fulfilled?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering them began to say, Take heed lest any one mislead you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For many shall come in my name, saying, It is I, and shall mislead many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars, be not disturbed, for [this] must happen, but the end is not yet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For nation shall rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; and there shall be earthquakes in [different] places, and there shall be famines and troubles: these things [are the] beginnings of throes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But ye, take heed to yourselves, for they shall deliver you up to sanhedrims and to synagogues: ye shall be beaten and brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the gospel must first be preached to all the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when they shall lead you away to deliver you up, be not careful beforehand as to what ye shall say, [nor prepare your discourse]: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak; for ye are not the speakers, but the Holy Spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But brother shall deliver up brother to death, and father child; and children shall rise up against parents, and cause them to be put to death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye will be hated of all on account of my name; but he that has endured to the end, he shall be saved.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not, (he that reads let him consider [it],) then let those in Judaea flee to the mountains;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and him that is upon the housetop not come down into the house, nor enter [into it] to take away anything out of his house;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and him that is in the field not return back to take his garment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But woe to those that are with child and to those that give suck in those days!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And pray that it may not be in winter time;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for those days shall be distress such as there has not been the like since [the] beginning of creation whichGod created, until now, and never shall be;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and if [the] Lord had not cut short those days, no flesh should have been saved; but on account of the elect whom he has chosen, he has cut short those days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And then if any one say to you, Lo, here [is] the Christ, or Lo, there, believe [it] not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For false Christs and false prophets will arise, and give signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, even the elect.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But do ye take heed: behold, I have told you all things beforehand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in those days, after that distress, the sun shall be darkened and the moon shall not give its light;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the stars of heaven shall be falling down, and the powers which are in the heavens shall be shaken;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and then shall they see the Son of man coming in clouds with great power and glory;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and then shall he send his angels and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from end of earth to end of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But learn the parable from the fig-tree: when its branch already becomes tender and puts forth the leaves, ye know that the summer is near.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus also ye, when ye see these things happening, know that it is near, at the doors.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say unto you, This generation shall in no wise pass away, till all these things take place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The heaven and the earth shall pass away, but my words shall in no wise pass away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But of that day or of that hour no one knows, neither the angels who are in heaven, nor the Son, but the Father.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Take heed, watch and pray, for ye do not know when the time is:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [it is] as a man gone out of the country, having left his house and given to his bondmen the authority, and to each one his work, and commanded the doorkeeper that he should watch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Watch therefore, for ye do not know when the master of the house comes: evening, or midnight, or cock-crow, or morning;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But what I say to you, I say to all, Watch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the passover and the [feast of] unleavened bread was after two days. And the chief priests and the scribes were seeking how they might seize him by subtlety and kill him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they said, Not in the feast, lest perhaps there be a tumult of the people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper, as he lay at table, there came a woman having an alabaster flask of ointment of pure nard, very costly; and having broken the alabaster flask, she poured it out upon his head.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were some indignant in themselves, and saying, Why has this waste been made of the ointment?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for this ointment could have been sold for more than three hundred denarii and given to the poor. And they spoke very angrily at her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus said, Let her alone; why do ye trouble her? she has wrought a good work as to me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for ye have the poor always with you, and whenever ye would ye can do them good; but me ye have not always.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What she could she has done. She has beforehand anointed my body for the burial.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And verily I say unto you, Wheresoever these glad tidings may be preached in the whole world, what this [woman] has done shall be also spoken of for a memorial of her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Judas Iscariote, one of the twelve, went away to the chief priests that he might deliver him up to them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they, when they heard it, rejoiced, and promised him to give money. And he sought how he could opportunely deliver him up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the first day of unleavened bread, when they slew the passover, his disciples say to him, Where wilt thou that we go and prepare, that thou mayest eat the passover?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he sends two of his disciples, and says to them, Go into the city, and a man shall meet you carrying a pitcher of water; follow him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And wheresoever he enters, say to the master of the house, The Teacher says, Where is my guest-chamber where I may eat the passover with my disciples?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and he will shew you a large upper room furnished ready. There make ready for us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And his disciples went away and came into the city, and found as he had said to them; and they made ready the passover.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when evening was come, he comes with the twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they lay at table and were eating, Jesus said, Verily I say to you, One of you shall deliver me up; he who is eating with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they began to be grieved, and to say to him, one by one, Is it I? [and another, Is it I?]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he answered and said to them, One of the twelve, he who dips with me in the dish.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Son of man goes indeed as it is written concerning him, but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is delivered up; [it were] good for that man if he had not been born.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as they were eating, Jesus, having taken bread, when he had blessed, broke [it], and gave [it] to them, and said, Take [this]: this is my body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having taken [the] cup, when he had given thanks, he gave [it] to them, and they all drank out of it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, This is my blood, that of the [new] covenant, that shed for many.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Verily I say to you, I will no more drink at all of the fruit of the vine, until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having sung a hymn, they went out to the mount of Olives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus says to them, All ye shall be offended, for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered abroad.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But after I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Peter said to him, Even if all should be offended, yet not I.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus says to him, Verily I say to thee, that thou to-day, in this night, before [the] cock shall crow twice, thou shalt thrice deny me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he said [so much] exceedingly the more, If I should have to die with thee, I will in no wise deny thee. And likewise said they all too.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they come to a place of which the name [is] Gethsemane, and he says to his disciples, Sit here while I shall pray.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he takes with him Peter and James and John, and he began to be amazed and oppressed in spirit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he says to them, My soul is full of grief even unto death; abide here and watch.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, going forward a little, he fell upon the earth; and he prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass away from him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said, Abba, Father, all things are possible to thee: take away this cup from me; but not what I will, but what thou [wilt].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he comes and finds them sleeping. And he says to Peter, Simon, dost thou sleep? Hast thou not been able to watch one hour?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Watch and pray, that ye enter not into temptation. The spirit indeed [is] willing, but the flesh weak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And going away, he prayed again, saying the same thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And returning, he found them again sleeping, for their eyes were heavy; and they knew not what they should answer him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he comes the third time and says to them, Sleep on now, and take your rest. It is enough; the hour is come; behold, the Son of man is delivered up into the hands of sinners.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, let us go; behold, he that delivers me up has drawn nigh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately, while he was yet speaking, Judas comes up, [being] one of the twelve, and with him a great crowd, with swords and sticks, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now he that delivered him up had given them a sign between them, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that is he; seize him, and lead [him] away safely.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And being come, straightway coming up to him, he says, Rabbi, Rabbi; and he covered him with kisses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they laid their hands upon him and seized him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But a certain one of those who stood by, having drawn his sword, struck the bondman of the high priest, and took off his ear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus answering said to them, Are ye come out as against a robber, with swords and sticks to take me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I was daily with you teaching in the temple, and ye did not seize me; but [it is] that the scriptures may be fulfilled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all left him and fled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And a certain young man followed him with a linen cloth cast about his naked [body]; and [the young men] seize him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he, leaving the linen cloth behind [him], fled from them naked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they led away Jesus to the high priest. And there come together to him all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter followed him at a distance, till [he was] within the court of the high priest's palace; and he was sitting with the officers and warming himself in the light [of the fire].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests and the whole sanhedrim sought testimony against Jesus to cause him to be put to death, and did not find [any].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For many bore false witness against him, and their testimony did not agree.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And certain persons rose up and bore false witness against him, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We heard him saying, I will destroy this temple which is made with hands, and in the course of three days I will build another not made with hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And neither thus did their testimony agree.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the high priest, rising up before them all, asked Jesus, saying, Answerest thou nothing? What do these testify against thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he was silent, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, and says to him, Thou art the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus said, I am, and ye shall see the Son of man sitting at the right hand of power, and coming with the clouds of heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the high priest, having rent his clothes, says, What need have we any more of witnesses?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have heard the blasphemy; what think ye? And they all condemned him to be guilty of death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some began to spit upon him, and cover up his face, and buffet him, and say to him, Prophesy; and the officers struck him with the palms of their hands.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Peter being below in the palace-court, there comes one of the maids of the high priest,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and seeing Peter warming himself, having looked at him, says, And thou wast with the Nazarene, Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he denied, saying, I know not nor understand what thou sayest. And he went out into the vestibule; and a cock crew.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the maid, seeing him, again began to say to those that stood by, This is [one] of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he again denied. And again, after a little, those that stood by said to Peter, Truly thou art [one] of them, for also thou art a Galilean.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he began to curse and to swear, I know not this man of whom ye speak.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the second time a cock crew. And Peter remembered the word that Jesus said to him, Before [the] cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice; and when he thought thereon he wept.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And immediately in the morning the chief priests, having taken counsel with the elders and scribes and the whole sanhedrim, bound Jesus and carried [him] away, and delivered [him] up to Pilate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answered and said to him, Thou sayest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief priests accused him urgently.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate asked him again, saying, Answerest thou nothing? See of how many things they bear witness against thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Jesus still answered nothing, so that Pilate marvelled.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But at [the] feast he released to them one prisoner, whomsoever they begged [of him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now there was the [person] named Barabbas bound with those who had made insurrection with [him], [and] that had committed murder in the insurrection.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the crowd crying out began to beg [that he would do] to them as he had always done.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Pilate answered them saying, Will ye that I release to you the King of the Jews?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he knew that the chief priests had delivered him up through envy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd that he might rather release Barabbas to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate answering said to them again, What will ye then that I do [to him] whom ye call King of the Jews?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they cried out again, Crucify him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate said to them, What evil then has he done? But they cried out the more urgently, Crucify him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate, desirous of contenting the crowd, released to them Barabbas, and delivered up Jesus, when he had scourged him, that he might be crucified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the soldiers led him away into the court which is [called the] praetorium, and they call together the whole band.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they clothe him with purple, and bind round on him a crown of thorns which they had plaited.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they struck his head with a reed, and spat on him, and, bending the knee, did him homage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they had mocked him, they took the purple off him, and put his own clothes on him; and they lead him out that they may crucify him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they compel to go [with them] a certain passer-by, Simon, a Cyrenian, coming from the field, the father of Alexander and Rufus, that he might carry his cross.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they bring him to the place [called] Golgotha, which, being interpreted, is Place of a skull.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they offered him wine [to drink] medicated with myrrh; but he did not take [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having crucified him, they part his clothes amongst [themselves], casting lots on them, what each one should take.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the superscription of what he was accused of was written up: The King of the Jews.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with him they crucify two robbers, one on his right hand, and one on his left.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [And the scripture was fulfilled which says, And he was reckoned with the lawless.]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they that passed by reviled him, shaking their heads, and saying, Aha, thou that destroyest the temple and buildest it in three days,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: save thyself, and descend from the cross.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In like manner the chief priests also, with the scribes, mocking with one another, said, He saved others; himself he cannot save.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let the Christ the King of Israel descend now from the cross, that we may see and may believe. And they that were crucified with him reproached him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when [the] sixth hour was come, there came darkness over the whole land until [the] ninth hour;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and at the ninth hour, Jesus cried with a loud voice, [saying], Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani? which is, being interpreted, MyGod, myGod, why hast thou forsaken me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of those who stood by, when they heard [it], said, Behold, he calls for Elias.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And one, running and filling a sponge with vinegar, fixed it on a reed, and gave him to drink, saying, Let alone, let us see if Elias comes to take him down.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jesus, having uttered a loud cry, expired.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the veil of the temple was rent in two from the top to the bottom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the centurion who stood by over against him, when he saw that he had expired having thus cried out, said, Truly this man was Son ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there were women also looking on from afar off, among whom were both Mary of Magdala, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him and ministered to him; and many others who came up with him to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when it was already evening, since it was [the] preparation, that is, [the day] before a sabbath,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Joseph of Arimathaea, an honourable councillor, who also himself was awaiting the kingdom ofGod, coming, emboldened himself and went in to Pilate and begged the body of Jesus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Pilate wondered if he were already dead; and having called to [him] the centurion, he inquired of him if he had long died.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when he knew from the centurion, he granted the body to Joseph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And having bought fine linen, [and] having taken him down, he swathed him in the fine linen, and laid him in a sepulchre which was cut out of rock, and rolled a stone to the door of the sepulchre.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Mary of Magdala and Mary the [mother] of Joses saw where he was put.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the sabbath being [now] past, Mary of Magdala, and Mary the [mother] of James, and Salome, bought aromatic spices that they might come and embalm him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And very early on the first [day] of the week they come to the sepulchre, the sun having risen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to one another, Who shall roll us away the stone out of the door of the sepulchre?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when they looked, they see that the stone has been rolled [away], for it was very great.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And entering into the sepulchre, they saw a young man sitting on the right, clothed in a white robe, and they were amazed and alarmed;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he says to them, Be not alarmed. Ye seek Jesus, the Nazarene, the crucified one. He is risen, he is not here; behold the place where they had put him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, he goes before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him, as he said to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they went out, and fled from the sepulchre. And trembling and excessive amazement possessed them, and they said nothing to any one, for they were afraid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now when he had risen very early, the first [day] of the week, he appeared first to Mary of Magdala, out of whom he had cast seven demons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She went and brought word to those that had been with him, [who were] grieving and weeping.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when these heard that he was alive and had been seen of her, they disbelieved [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after these things he was manifested in another form to two of them as they walked, going into the country;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they went and brought word to the rest; neither did they believe them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Afterwards as they lay at table he was manifested to the eleven, and reproached [them with] their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they had not believed those who had seen him risen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to them, Go into all the world, and preach the glad tidings to all the creation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He that believes and is baptised shall be saved, and he that disbelieves shall be condemned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these signs shall follow those that have believed: in my name they shall cast out demons; they shall speak with new tongues;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they shall take up serpents; and if they should drink any deadly thing it shall not injure them; they shall lay hands upon the infirm, and they shall be well.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord therefore, after he had spoken to them, was taken up into heaven, and sat at the right hand ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they, going forth, preached everywhere, the Lord working with [them], and confirming the word by the signs following upon [it].
